titleStmt.title: Letter from James Brown to Sir Malachy Kelly, 10 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: James Brown

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from solicitor James Brown to Malachy Kelly asking for a reply concering the case of the death of Elizabeth Hogg.Sir Malachy J. Kelly (1851-1916) was the Chief Crown Solicitor for Ireland. He was appointed Crown Solicitor for Mayo in 1884 and was involved in a number of high profile cases. In 1905, he was appointed Chief Crown Solicitor for Ireland and moved to Dublin.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/3888

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
BROAD STREET, MAGHERAFELT 10th March 1916 Dear Sir, Elizabeth Hogg deceased I wrote you herein some time ago
but got no reply and I would thank you to
give this matter your earliest consideration. Yours faithfully James Brown Sir Malachy J Kelly Chief Crown Solicitor for Ireland The CastladdrLine> Dublin.
